paper_title: Unified Preference Optimization: Language Model Alignment Beyond the Preference Frontier

traditional_approach.name: Reinforcement Learning via Human Feedback (RLHF)
process: Train reward models using maximum likelihood estimation (MLE)
Apply reinforcement learning (typically Proximal Policy Optimization - PPO) to maximize rewards
limitations: Time concerns due to generation through sampling from the LM

recent_approach.name: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO)
process: Reframing and reparameterizing RLHF objective with Bradley-Terry or Plackett-Luce preference models
Trains significantly faster than on-policy RL techniques
extensions: Generalizations to arbitrary preference optimization objectives
limitations: Cannot directly minimize unsafe content or control text reading level in a sample-efficient manner

proposed_solution.name: Hybrid Preference Optimization (HPO)
process: Leverages simplicity of DPO for maximizing preference alignment
Allows arbitrary auxiliary objectives with offline RL
Uses simple weighted maximum likelihood objective
advantages: More stable and efficient during training compared to prior work using PPO
More theoretically principled than techniques using PPO for offline RL
Can minimize important auxiliary objectives including various forms of toxicity and reading level

results.performance: Equals or surpasses overall performance achieved by KTO, offline PPO, and DPO
results.implementation: Requires roughly ten additional lines of model code on top of KTO
results.capabilities: Ability to minimize important auxiliary objectives, including various forms of toxicity and reading level

title: Unified Preference Optimization: Language Model Alignment Beyond the Preference Frontier
